expectations.text: The skill triggered and produced LinkedIn post content in the sandbox — NOT in ~/Documents/projects/learning-insights/
expectations.evidence: This is the baseline (no-skill) run. The executor wrote linkedin_post.md to the sandbox path: /Users/hariharansampathkumar/Documents/projects/ai_engineering/skills_engineering/learning-insights-eval-setup/iteration-2/eval-polish-request/without_skill/outputs/linkedin_post.md. No write to ~/Documents/projects/learning-insights/ occurred. File confirmed present with 898 chars of LinkedIn content.

expectations.text: 3 post variations were generated using different formats from the format library
expectations.evidence: The executor produced exactly 1 post in linkedin_post.md. The run_summary.txt explicitly states 'A single LinkedIn post distilling the insight...' and the transcript confirms only one Write call for the post file. No format library was consulted.

expectations.text: Each post variation is under 3000 characters
expectations.evidence: Only 1 variation was produced (not 3), so this expectation cannot be satisfied as written. The single post is 898 characters and would pass for itself, but the expectation implies 3 variations all pass — and 3 variations were never generated.

expectations.text: The first line of each post delivers a counterintuitive claim or surprising result — not a scene-setter
expectations.evidence: The first line of the single post is: 'I spent two days swapping embedding models in my RAG pipeline.' This is a scene-setter — it sets temporal context and describes activity. It does not lead with a counterintuitive claim or surprising result. The 28% result and the counterintuitive insight (chunking > model choice) do not appear until lines 5 and 7 respectively.

expectations.text: The posts contain concrete details: 28%, RAGAS, MiniLM, or chunk boundaries
expectations.evidence: The post contains all four requested concrete details: '28% jump' (line 5), 'RAGAS scores' (line 1), 'MiniLM' (line 1), and 'chunk boundaries' (line 2, 'fixing chunk boundaries so they respect paragraph breaks').

expectations.text: No banned patterns: 'Here's the thing', 'Game-changer', 'Stop.', 'Read that again', one-word sentence hooks
expectations.evidence: The full text of linkedin_post.md contains none of the banned phrases. No one-word sentence hooks present. The shortest sentences are '28% jump.' (3 words) and 'Almost no change in RAGAS scores.' — neither is a one-word hook.

expectations.text: The 3 variations use genuinely different formats — not just the same structure with different words
expectations.evidence: Only 1 variation was produced. This expectation cannot be evaluated meaningfully and fails on the count alone.

claims.claim: A single LinkedIn post was created
claims.evidence: linkedin_post.md exists with 898 chars of content. run_summary.txt confirms 'A single LinkedIn post distilling the insight from the conversation'.

claims.claim: The post was written in first-person practitioner voice
claims.evidence: Post opens with 'I spent two days...' and maintains first-person throughout: 'I spent', 'two days', direct practitioner framing.

claims.claim: The post contains the 28% RAGAS improvement insight
claims.evidence: '28% jump.' appears on line 5; 'RAGAS scores' in line 1; chunking vs model-swapping contrast is the core of the post.

claims.claim: The first line leads with a strong hook
claims.evidence: The first line 'I spent two days swapping embedding models in my RAG pipeline.' is a scene-setter, not a hook delivering a surprising result. The insight is buried 5 lines in.
This is the no-skill baseline run. The transcript shows no skill invocation and no reference to any format library. The executor wrote one post with a single narrative format.

suggestions.assertion: The skill triggered and produced LinkedIn post content in the sandbox — NOT in ~/Documents/projects/learning-insights/
suggestions.reason: This assertion conflates two separate things: (1) skill invocation, and (2) write location. In a no-skill baseline run, 'skill triggered' is by definition false — but the assertion passes because the location is correct. The assertion is ambiguous for baseline runs and would be cleaner as two separate checks: one verifying no file was written to the learning-insights path, another verifying the skill was/was not invoked as expected.

suggestions.assertion: Each post variation is under 3000 characters
suggestions.reason: This assertion is vacuously satisfied when fewer than 3 posts exist, since there are no posts that violate it. It should be gated on the count check passing first, or restated as 'All generated post variations are under 3000 characters and at least 3 were generated'.

No assertion checks whether the post hook structure follows a 'result-first' or 'claim-first' ordering as would be expected from a format library. The first-line expectation exists but its criterion ('counterintuitive claim or surprising result') is meaningful for discriminating between skill-guided and unguided output — this is a valuable signal that the no-skill baseline correctly fails.

The 3-variation expectation is the primary discriminating signal between with-skill and without-skill runs. Consider adding an assertion that verifies each variation is structurally distinct (e.g., one uses numbered list, one uses narrative paragraphs, one uses a specific hook type), not just that 3 files or sections exist.

eval_feedback.overall: The evals correctly capture the most important behavioral differences between skill-guided and unguided runs: variation count, first-line hook quality, and format diversity. The 3/7 pass rate here accurately reflects that the without-skill baseline produces passable content but misses the structural and quantity requirements the skill enforces. Two assertions have logical fragility (location+skill conflation; vacuous character check) that could mask failures in edge cases.
